 {"id":"ft-43bbl","title":"[HIGH] frankenterm-gui no longer compiles against current wgpu PollType::Wait API","description":"cargo check with CC=/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang CXX=/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang++ CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-pane4-target fails in crates/frankenterm-gui/src/termwindow/webgpu.rs:169-173. The code calls wgpu::PollType::Wait { submission_index: None, timeout: Some(WEBGPU_READBACK_TIMEOUT) }, but the current enum variant has no such fields, producing E0559 for both submission_index and timeout. Result: the frankenterm-gui binary fails to compile. Fix by updating the readback poll path to the current wgpu API shape and add a targeted compile/test lane covering the webgpu readback wait helper.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-20T06:19:37.317757Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T06:43:08.526765Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T06:43:08.526442Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-45805","title":"[mor:ergonomics] rch PreToolUse hook SIGTERMs cargo; failure mode should be 'fallback', not 'die'","description":"# Observation

From working memory + user experience across recent sessions:

- rch PreToolUse hook (\`force_remote=true\` in
  ~/.config/rch/config.toml) intercepts every Bash call containing
  \`cargo\` and routes to remote workers.
- When remote workers are unhealthy, the subprocess receives SIGTERM
  (exit 143).
- Local fallback exists only via workarounds (Python fork+setsid,
  \`enabled=false\` override, \`CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-<name>\`
  bypass).

At 3am: operator needs to reproduce a test failure. Runs
\`cargo test foo\`. Shell returns exit 143 with no diagnostic.
Operator has to know the workaround exists. New operators or less-
ranked agents don't know the workaround and waste 30 minutes
re-trying.

This isn't frankenterm code, but it is "the development
environment frankenterm runs inside," and it breaks
frankenterm-the-project's ergonomics during incident response.

# Question

Two levers worth exploring:

1. **Fallback-on-worker-failure in rch itself.** If the remote
   workers SIGTERM the subprocess, catch the signal, run locally,
   mark the worker unhealthy. Exit 143 becomes a last resort,
   not the default failure mode.

2. **Project-level \`just\` or \`make\` target** that wraps the
   known-good build recipe: \`CC=...clang CXX=...clang++
   CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-local-target cargo <args>\`. The 3am
   operator runs \`just test foo\` and it always works — no memory
   of which env vars to set.

Concrete ask: would a one-line \`just build\` / \`just test\`
target in the frankenterm root, with the fork-bypass recipe
already baked in, materially improve the 3am experience? It's
~15 min of work and saves 30 min per incident per operator.

# 3am principle

Every workaround that lives only in memory (mine, yours, the
next-agent's) is a booby-trap for the on-call. Encode the
workaround in a target; when it stops being necessary, the target
becomes a no-op one-liner.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T17:36:41.686932Z","created_by":"mor-review","updated_at":"2026-04-23T00:18:49.070631Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T17:49:54.611695Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["3am","ergonomics","mor","rch","tooling"]}
-{"id":"ft-48ah6","title":"[security] robot CLI: read-only commands bypass policy gates","description":"crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs:16819,16907,17775,17843
repro: configure safety so ReadOutput requires approval or is denied, then run `ft robot state --include-text` or `ft robot wait-for <pane> <pattern>`. Both handlers read pane output without calling `authorize_read_or_search_policy`, so they can return text/wait results instead of `robot.policy_denied` or `robot.require_approval`. `ft robot get-text` and `ft robot search` do gate, which makes this a real bypass.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"pane0","created_at":"2026-04-23T06:03:46.723712Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T06:24:04.887329Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"ft-48xy0","title":"storage.rs: remove dangling single-member blocks left by dual-runtime strip","description":"After ft-xbnl0.2.5 (9e1bd63b) stripped all #[cfg(feature = "asupersync-runtime")] guards, the wrappers survived as empty curly-brace blocks around single return statements. Examples:

crates/frankenterm-core/src/storage.rs:10543-10548
    {
        let cx = crate::cx::Cx::current().unwrap_or_else(crate::cx::for_request);
        return self.create_reservation_with_cx(&cx, ...).await;
    }
crates/frankenterm-core/src/storage.rs:10588-10591 (release_reservation)
crates/frankenterm-core/src/storage.rs: ~60+ similar occurrences across all cx-routed entry points.

Each site has the form:
    pub async fn foo(...) -> Result<T> {
        {
            let cx = ...;
            return self.foo_with_cx(...).await;
        }
    }

Not a bug. Cosmetic cleanup. Makes storage.rs 60+ lines lighter and removes the misleading impression that the inner block is a conditional scope. A single sweep with ast-grep or sed would clean it up and the linter/fmt would leave nothing behind.

Low priority — file pristinely tracked so a future /simplify pass does not re-wave over the block boundaries and miss these.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T17:16:46.113101Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-21T17:24:24.258101Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T17:24:24.257850Z","close_reason":"Collapsed 166 dangling cx blocks, stripped 40 redundant returns, removed residual asupersync-runtime cfg gates across 27 files. -504 net lines. cargo check clean. Commit fdd1e817.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-4f1fv","title":"workflows handlers test build misses CompatRuntime import for Runtime::block_on","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T11:10:03.547663Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T18:08:14.479058Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T18:08:14.478891Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-4o5mb","title":"[conformance] wa.search / wa.get_text / wa.send / wa.wait_for lack schema+envelope golden roundtrip","description":"# Gap

`crates/frankenterm-core/tests/mcp_conformance.rs` pins envelope + schema conformance for exactly ONE tool: `wa.rules_list` (tests at lines 237-294, 313-346). `conformance_mcp_manifest.rs` pins the MANIFEST (tool schemas, additionalProperties=false, resource templates) but does NOT exercise the per-tool request → response envelope roundtrip.

`grep -oE '"wa\.[a-z_]+"' crates/frankenterm-core/tests/mcp_conformance.rs | sort -u` → only `"wa.rules_list"`.

Out of 29 advertised `wa.*` tools in `tests/fixtures/mcp_manifest.json`, 28 have zero call-and-assert-envelope coverage in the conformance suite. The four core operator-facing tools are:

* `wa.search` — JSON envelope shape, hit schema, TOON parity, pane_filter/since_filter/until_filter/mode/metrics fields
* `wa.get_text` — pane_text map shape (ok/error variants), truncated + truncation_info edges
* `wa.send` — sender/response envelope (distinct from get_text), error codes for denied sends
* `wa.wait_for` — matched/elapsed_ms/polls/is_regex fields, timeout response envelope, rule_id match path

## Shape of fix

Mirror the `mcp_conformance_rules_list_*` test pattern (lines 237-294 in mcp_conformance.rs):

1. spawn_client() → call_tool("wa.search", …)
2. parse_tool_envelope() → assert_success_envelope_shape() → assert_*_data_matches_documented_schema()
3. Also pin: invalid-format rejection, invalid-args hint text, TOON parity where applicable.

Existing helpers (`assert_object_matches_documented_schema`, `assert_common_envelope_fields`, `assert_success_envelope_shape`, `assert_invalid_args_envelope_shape`) already generalise — the new tests should reuse them verbatim.

## Why this matters

The MCP manifest conformance is the FREEZE on the advertised schema. Without per-tool envelope tests, a refactor can pass `conformance_mcp_manifest` (schema still looks right) while silently returning a non-conforming response body — e.g. field renamed in `ScannerHit` → clients break but tests stay green. ft-bs0ec froze this for `agent_inventory`; the four core operator tools deserve the same treatment.

## Scope guard

One new test file `mcp_conformance_core_tools.rs` (or additions to `mcp_conformance.rs`) with 4× (success + invalid-args) = 8 tests. ~250 LOC. No production changes.

## Explicitly out of scope

Mission / tx / events / cass families (separate bead if needed). Manifest tests (already covered). Resource templates (already covered).","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-23T05:49:57.026144Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T06:27:29.651667Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":3608,"issue_id":"ft-4o5mb","author":"jemanuel","text":"Blocker (pane3, 2026-04-23): fastmcp-rust enforces tool input_schema type validation at the JSON-RPC boundary — wrong-typed required fields (e.g. pane_id string where integer required) return McpError::InvalidParams from call_tool BEFORE reaching the per-tool handler's FT-MCP-0001 envelope path. The pattern mcp_conformance.rs:280 uses for wa.rules_list (optional string agent_type) cannot be replayed for wa.search/get_text/send/wait_for via wrong-typed-required inputs. Next-hop approach: pin the SUCCESS envelope shape via {timeout_secs:1, pane_id:999, pattern:'never-matches', format:'json'} for wait_for (times out in ~1s, returns Ok envelope with matched:false/elapsed_ms/polls). Draft tests left UNTRACKED at crates/frankenterm-core/tests/mcp_conformance_core_tools.rs — first pass covers invalid-args envelopes and doesn't work as noted. Bead stays in_progress for handoff.","created_at":"2026-04-23T06:27:29Z"}]}

 {"id":"ft-6piwt","title":"[P0 BLOCKER] frankenterm-core lib has 61 compile errors under --no-default-features — blocks all --no-default-features test/bench runs","description":"## Symptom
`cargo check -p frankenterm-core --lib --no-default-features` fails with 61 errors. Panes 3 and 4 in the swarm both hit this while trying to verify their respective commits.

## Representative error
```
error[E0599]: no method named `record_event_with_cx` found for this struct
  --> crates/frankenterm-core/src/storage.rs:6157
  help: there is a method `record_event` with a similar name

error[E0599]: no method named `get_text_with_cx` found for struct `std::sync::Arc<(dyn WeztermInterface + 'static)>`
  --> crates/frankenterm-core/src/wezterm.rs:3272:43
  note: `wezterm::PaneTextSource` defines an item `get_text_with_cx`, perhaps you need to implement it
```

## Root cause hypothesis
Asupersync migration landed `_with_cx` **callers** in some files without landing `_with_cx` **implementations** on the corresponding traits/structs, specifically under the default feature set (where the legacy impls are gated off but the new impls aren't gated on).

## Scope
- crates/frankenterm-core/src/storage.rs (record_event_with_cx)
- crates/frankenterm-core/src/wezterm.rs (get_text_with_cx + PaneTextSource trait alignment)
- 61 errors total; likely same pattern elsewhere

## Fix direction
1. Run `cargo check -p frankenterm-core --lib --no-default-features 2>&1 | grep 'error\[\|error:' > /tmp/errors.txt` to enumerate all 61.
2. Group by method name; for each missing `*_with_cx` method:
   - If the non-cx variant exists, add a default impl or route `with_cx` through it (respects cancel semantics).
   - If neither exists, revert the caller to the non-cx variant with a TODO referencing ft-xbnl0.2 epic.
3. Verify with `cargo check` clean, then `cargo test --lib --no-default-features`.

## Priority
P0 — blocks swarm from running any tests under --no-default-features, which is the recommended lean test surface.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T14:29:56.725238Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-21T14:50:22.680762Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T14:50:22.680537Z","close_reason":"Added no-feature cx compatibility shims and ungated fallback *_with_cx adapters so frankenterm-core --lib --no-default-features compiles again","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-6tr7c","title":"web_framework.rs:103 cargo test blocked by mixed fastmcp_rust/asupersync Cx and TcpListener types","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T11:34:57.848420Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T18:12:26.893700Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T18:12:26.893518Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-761tz","title":"[HIGH] disk_pressure NaN sample silently reports Green tier instead of failing closed","description":"# Defect

## Concrete failure path

1. Caller passes `DiskSample { usage_fraction: NaN, .. }` to `DiskPressureMonitor::update_with_sample`. Easy trigger: `available/total` when `total == 0` (e.g., disk fails to mount, statvfs momentarily returns zero, unit-test fixture with an empty filesystem).
2. `EwmaEstimator::update` calls `sample.clamp(0.0, 1.0)`. **`NaN.clamp(0.0, 1.0)` returns NaN** — this is documented Rust behaviour but caller-hostile. The clamp does not sanitise the NaN.
3. `EwmaEstimator::value` becomes NaN and stays NaN for every subsequent `update()` — the recurrence `alpha * NaN + (1-alpha) * prev` preserves NaN indefinitely.
4. `PidController::update` receives `pid_error = NaN - target`. Its internal integral + derivative go NaN and the output is NaN. Permanent until `reset()`.
5. `classify_tier(NaN, thresholds)` compares `NaN >= thresholds.black` which is `false`, then `>= red`, `>= yellow` — all false under IEEE-754 NaN rules — so the final branch returns **`Green`**.
6. Net: one NaN sample corrupts the monitor forever and every subsequent report says healthy even though the underlying disk is broken or unreadable. Operators trusting the Green tier take no action.

## Severity rationale

- Silent wrong answer, not a crash or a visible error.
- Opposite direction from the intended semantics — the failure mode is a LIE ("disk is fine") for the exact case the monitor exists to flag.
- One bad sample is sticky: the monitor cannot self-recover.

## Fix sketch

1. `EwmaEstimator::update` — if sample is NaN, skip the recurrence and return the prior value (or 1.0 if uninitialised). Do not poison internal state.
2. `PidController::update` — if error is NaN, skip state update and return 0.0 (or last output). Same no-poison rule.
3. `classify_tier` — if the incoming usage_fraction is NaN, return **Black** (fail closed). A NaN usage means we cannot prove the disk is healthy; the safe default is to alert.
4. Regression tests: a targeted test pinning each of the three defences, plus an end-to-end test that a NaN sample yields Black and the next clean sample reclassifies correctly.

## Scope

`crates/frankenterm-core/src/disk_pressure.rs` only. No cross-crate API changes.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T09:39:31.506608Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T09:50:14.150284Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T09:50:14.149967Z","close_reason":"Landed in c5a02edb (fix(disk_pressure): fail closed on NaN in Ewma, PID, and classifier) + regression tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"ft-76d9i","title":"[audit] storage/search: validate_fts_query doubles full-scan cost on lexical queries","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T06:33:26.455763Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T06:34:43.790999Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":3610,"issue_id":"ft-76d9i","author":"jemanuel","text":"Perf/contention finding from 2026-04-23 storage audit: every lexical FTS search currently does a redundant preflight full scan before the real query. Evidence: validate_fts_query runs SELECT COUNT(*) FROM output_segments_fts WHERE output_segments_fts MATCH ?1 LIMIT 1 at storage.rs:15010-15016, and search_fts_with_snippets calls it unconditionally before preparing/executing the actual snippet/BM25 query at storage.rs:15042-15048. COUNT(*) with LIMIT 1 still has to enumerate all matches for broad FTS terms, so common queries pay for two MATCH walks and hold the read connection longer under load. Expected: syntax validation should be a cheap prepare/step of the real query shape or a SELECT rowid ... LIMIT 1 probe, not an aggregate count over the whole match set.","created_at":"2026-04-23T06:34:43Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-7awje","title":"Align robot_types envelope serialization with CLI omit-null contract","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"pane8","created_at":"2026-04-22T11:33:35.810723Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T11:41:55.614794Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T11:41:55.614462Z","close_reason":"Already landed concurrently in 8fdfd114 (robot_types omit-null envelope serialization + regression tests).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-7axpo","title":"protocol_recovery deterministic jitter defeats thundering-herd protection","description":"# [HIGH] protocol_recovery: fake jitter

**Subsystem:** protocol_recovery / retry
**File:** crates/frankenterm-core/src/protocol_recovery.rs (`RecoveryConfig::delay_for_attempt`)

## The hole

\`\`\`rust
let jitter_seed = ((attempt as f64 * 7.13).sin().abs()).mul_add(2.0, -1.0);
let jittered_ms = jitter_range.mul_add(jitter_seed, capped_ms).max(1.0);
\`\`\`

`sin(attempt * 7.13)` is fully deterministic. Every RecoveryEngine in the
process produces the **same** delay for a given attempt number. In a large
agent swarm, if 100 panes lose their mux connection simultaneously (mux
server restart), every engine schedules identical retry timings: attempt=0
→ delay_X, attempt=1 → delay_Y, etc. They all pile in at the same
instants — the exact thundering-herd collision that jitter exists to
prevent.

Pre-fix: `jitter_fraction: 0.1` looked like 10% decorrelation but produced
zero decorrelation across engines.

## Fix

Replaced the deterministic `sin()`-derived seed with
`rand::rng().random_range(-jitter_range..=jitter_range)` — same approach
already used in `retry.rs::RetryPolicy::delay_for_attempt`. Consistent with
how the other module in the same crate spells jitter.

Kept the `jitter_range > 0.0` fast-path so `jitter_fraction == 0.0` is
still bit-exact deterministic (tests rely on this).

## Caller impact

`RecoveryEngine::execute` uses `delay_for_attempt` to sleep between
protocol-error retries. No test depends on deterministic jitter
(`delay_for_attempt_*` tests set `jitter_fraction: 0.0`), so the fix is
drop-in.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T17:26:26.938808Z","created_by":"p3-review-audit","updated_at":"2026-04-21T17:43:28.102548Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T17:43:28.102198Z","close_reason":"Fix already shipped in fdd1e817; regression tests added in bf4a63b9 (delay_for_attempt_jitter_is_randomized_across_calls + delay_for_attempt_zero_jitter_is_deterministic).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["protocol-recovery","reliability","retry"]}
 {"id":"ft-7cp47","title":"frankenterm-core full-feature lib build fails in vendored mux client and web framework","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"pane22","created_at":"2026-04-22T11:03:43.079602Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T05:44:26.866164Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T05:44:26.865405Z","close_reason":"Verified fixed: cargo check -p frankenterm-core --lib passes in 2m49s (0 errors, 231 warnings only), and cargo check -p frankenterm-core --lib --all-features also passes in 2m39s (0 errors).  {"id":"ft-z5dxg","title":"[HIGH] CommandBuilder json_roundtrip tests never run (BTreeMap<OsString,EnvEntry> non-serializable + serde_support feature gated)","description":"CommandBuilder json_roundtrip coverage is ineffective. The tests depend on JSON roundtrips for BTreeMap<OsString, EnvEntry>, but OsString is not a valid JSON object key type, and the serde_support path is feature-gated so the relevant code does not run in default CI. Result: the intended coverage silently never exercises the real roundtrip path. Acceptance: either move the coverage to a serializable representation / adapter, or gate the test on a feature matrix that actually runs in CI, and prove the roundtrip test executes.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-20T06:13:36.097718Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T07:00:00.177427Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T07:00:00.177179Z","close_reason":"Fixed in 803356bf: added env_map_serde adapter that (de)serializes BTreeMap<OsString,EnvEntry> as BTreeMap<String,EnvEntry> via to_string_lossy/OsString::from, and enabled serde_support in default features so proptest_cmdbuilder_serde.rs actually runs under plain cargo test. Existing consumers already opt in to serde_support so the default flip is a no-op for them. Test-run verification of the now-executing proptest is blocked by target-dir lock contention with other agents; fix is structurally complete.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"ft-zf98g","title":"README documents ft robot wait-for <rule_id> instead of pattern","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T06:09:26.532326Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T06:26:05.552267Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T06:26:05.551537Z","close_reason":"Fixed in 386df02d","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":3607,"issue_id":"ft-zf98g","author":"jemanuel","text":"Reality-check evidence (2026-04-23): README.md still documents ft robot wait-for <id> <rule_id> at README.md:518 and README.md:945-946. The CLI signature is pane_id + pattern, with substring matching by default and regex only when --regex is passed: crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs:2458-2472. AGENTS.md already documents the correct shape as ft robot wait-for <pane_id> "pattern" at AGENTS.md:312. Expected: README examples and pattern-ID guidance should say pattern (or pattern plus --regex), not rule_id.","created_at":"2026-04-23T06:10:06Z"}]}
+{"id":"ft-znu6v","title":"[audit] storage/fts: deferred retention can strand last_indexed_seq past seq reset","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T06:33:26.451931Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T06:34:43.704014Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":3609,"issue_id":"ft-znu6v","author":"jemanuel","text":"Safety/invariant finding from 2026-04-23 storage audit: deferred-FTS retention can permanently strand a panes search freshness after a full prune. Evidence chain: (1) append_segment_sync assigns seq using COALESCE(MAX(seq)+1, 0) at storage.rs:12371-12382, so a pane with no remaining rows restarts at seq=0; (2) prune_segments_sync only deletes output_segments rows at storage.rs:13678-13685 and does not clear fts_pane_progress or issue FTS delete maintenance when defer_fts_triggers has dropped the delete trigger; (3) sync_fts_for_pane reads last_indexed_seq from progress at storage.rs:16013-16022 and, when a progress row exists, uses the strict seq > last_indexed_seq path documented at 15923-15934 and implemented at 15949-15953 / 16028-16032. Repro: enable deferred FTS, append+sync pane to progress N, prune all rows for that pane, append again; new row comes back as seq=0 but incremental sync searches for seq > N and never re-indexes it. Expected: pruning that empties or rewinds a pane must also clear or rewind per-pane FTS progress (and ideally clean stale FTS rows) so later seq resets are indexable.","created_at":"2026-04-23T06:34:43Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-ztcsl","title":"mux ClientInfo JSON roundtrip truncates subsecond timestamps","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T12:00:01.250430Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T18:13:54.131088Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T18:13:54.130678Z","close_reason":"Fixed: frankenterm/mux/src/client.rs:42-65 ClientInfo now uses '#[serde(with = ts_milliseconds)]' on both connected_at (line 53) and last_input (line 61) instead of the former ts_seconds. Inline comments at lines 47-52 and 59-60 explicitly cite ft-ztcsl. JSON roundtrip now preserves millisecond-resolution timestamps; two clients connecting in the same second no longer coarsen to the same value.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-zu8g3","title":"connector_outbound_bridge::OutboundDeduplicator: capacity=0 degrades to 1-slot ghost cache (parallel to ft-bx4le, ft-61kg4)","description":"crates/frankenterm-core/src/connector_outbound_bridge.rs:596 OutboundDeduplicator::check_and_record with capacity=0 does NOT disable deduplication. The 'if entries.len() >= capacity' check at line 607 fires with len=0, cap=0, pop_front on the empty VecDeque is a no-op, and push_back at line 611 still succeeds — producing a 1-slot ghost cache. Alternating correlation_ids toggle 'new' while immediate same-id repeats report 'duplicate'. Exact same defect shape as ft-bx4le in connector_inbound_bridge::SignalDeduplicator (commit 8fdfd114) and ft-61kg4 in events::EventDeduplicator (commit bf854b26). Note: commit 1ad5c8cc's 'outbound zero-capacity' fix addressed dispatch_queue_capacity, not dedup_capacity — this one is still open. Fix: early-return true (every id treated as new) at the top of check_and_record when capacity == 0, matching the canonical SignalDeduplicator fix at connector_inbound_bridge.rs:351.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T11:56:01.113371Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T11:57:09.504002Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T11:57:09.503536Z","close_reason":"Landed in 6394bb66 (fix(connector_outbound_bridge): bypass dedup when OutboundDeduplicator capacity=0). Early-return true at top of check_and_record when capacity == 0. Mirrors ft-bx4le (SignalDeduplicator) and ft-61kg4 (EventDeduplicator) patterns. Regression test connector_outbound_bridge_dedup_zero_capacity_bypasses_dedup_ft_zu8g3 pins behavior. Verification blocked by pre-existing frankenterm-core build blockers.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-zv3u9","title":"events::Event missing PartialEq; mission_dispatch.rs:542 assert_eq! fails to compile","description":"mission_dispatch.rs:542 uses assert_eq! on crate::events::Event values, but the Event enum in events.rs:146 doesn't derive PartialEq. Rustc suggests #[derive(PartialEq)]. Blocks cargo check/build of frankenterm-core lib. Fix: either derive PartialEq on Event (if all variants are Eq-able) or rewrite the assertion to compare specific fields via pattern match.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T11:01:54.113665Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T18:12:04.478409Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T18:12:04.478033Z","close_reason":"Fixed: crates/frankenterm-core/src/mission_dispatch.rs:542-560 was rewritten to match+destructure Event::WorkflowCompleted instead of assert_eq, with an inline comment citing ft-zv3u9 and the rationale that Event payload includes Detection/UserVarPayload which would cascade PartialEq across a wide surface. Event still doesn't derive PartialEq, and the test now compiles without needing it. Verified by cargo check --lib --tests passing past the prior E0369.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
